skill.name: presentation-hook
skill.description: Creates engaging opening statements and powerful closings for medical presentations.
skill.summary: Generates attention-grabbing hooks and memorable conclusions for medical and academic presentations.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 42 patterns across 4 files (539 lines) with a computed risk score of 100/100. After contextual evaluation, all 42 findings are false positives: 26 'backtick execution' matches are Markdown code fences, 6 'weak cryptographic algorithm' matches are false triggers on words containing 'MD' (medical, recommendations), 6 'system reconnaissance' matches are generic documentation text, 2 'hardcoded URL' matches are a documentation source link, and 2 'path traversal' matches are security checklist items warning against traversal. The packaged Python script (scripts/main.py) is a benign 38-line string template generator with no network access, no filesystem operations, and no code execution beyond string formatting. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or hidden malicious behavior detected. Skill is safe to publish.

content.value_statement: Medical and academic presentations need strong openings to capture attention and powerful closings to leave a lasting impression. This skill generates tailored hooks and conclusions for any presentation topic and audience.
actual_capabilities: Generate attention-grabbing opening statements for presentations
Create memorable closing statements that reinforce key messages
Tailor hooks to specific audiences such as clinicians, researchers, or students
Produce alternative hook variations for comparison and selection
Follow a structured output format with primary hook and alternatives
Support both opening and closing hook types via a simple parameter
limitations: Uses template-based generation rather than deeply customized narrative hooks
Output quality depends on the clarity of the provided topic and audience
Limited to medical and academic presentation domains
Does not generate full presentation scripts, only openings and closings

use_cases.title: Conference Presentation Preparation
use_cases.description: A medical researcher needs a compelling opening for a conference talk on a clinical trial. The skill generates multiple hook options tailored to a specialist audience.

use_cases.title: Lecture Introduction Design
use_cases.description: A healthcare educator creating course materials needs engaging openings for each lecture module. The skill produces audience-appropriate hooks for students at different levels.

use_cases.title: Student Presentation Practice
use_cases.description: A medical student preparing a class presentation needs a strong opening and closing. The skill generates hooks that help the student structure their talk around a central message.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Opening Hook
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an opening hook for a presentation about diabetes management. The audience is clinical practitioners.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with basic hook generation

prompt_templates.title: Targeted Specialist Opening
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an engaging opening statement for a presentation on immunotherapy advances. The audience is oncology researchers familiar with current treatments.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generating hooks for a specialized expert audience

prompt_templates.title: Powerful Closing Statement
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a closing statement for a presentation about pediatric care innovations. The audience is hospital administrators and nursing staff. The closing should inspire action.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a memorable conclusion that calls the audience to action

prompt_templates.title: Multiple Alternatives with Tone
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate three alternative opening hooks for a presentation on AI in radiology. One should be data-driven, one should be story-driven, and one should pose a provocative question. The audience is a mixed group of technical and clinical professionals.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comparing different hook styles to find the best fit

output_examples.input: Topic: diabetes management, Audience: clinicians, Type: opening
output_examples.output: Primary hook: "What if I told you that diabetes management could change everything we know about patient care?" Alternatives: "Every year, thousands of patients face challenges with diabetes management." and "Imagine a world where diabetes management is no longer a barrier."

output_examples.input: Topic: immunotherapy advances, Audience: oncology researchers, Type: closing
output_examples.output: Primary hook: "Together, we can transform immunotherapy advances and improve patient outcomes." Alternatives: "The future of immunotherapy advances starts with the actions we take today." and "Thank you. Let us make a difference in immunotherapy advances."

output_examples.input: Topic: mental health screening, Audience: primary care physicians, Type: opening
output_examples.output: Primary hook: "What if I told you that mental health screening could change everything we know about patient care?" Alternatives: "Every year, thousands of patients face challenges with mental health screening." and "Imagine a world where mental health screening is no longer a barrier."

best_practices: Provide a clear and specific topic to get the most relevant hook output
Specify the audience type to ensure the hook matches the expected expertise level
Review generated alternatives and select the one that best fits your presentation style
anti_patterns: Do not use the generated hook verbatim without adapting it to your speaking style
Do not expect the skill to generate a full presentation script beyond openings and closings
Do not provide vague topics like 'healthcare' without specifying a focused subject area

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It generates opening statements and closing remarks for medical and academic presentations. You provide a topic, audience, and hook type, and it returns a primary hook with alternatives.

faq.question: What topics does this skill support?
faq.answer: It is designed for medical and healthcare topics but works with any academic or professional subject. The templates are most effective for scientific and clinical presentation contexts.

faq.question: Can I customize the tone or style of the hook?
faq.answer: The skill generates hooks using built-in templates. You can request specific tones such as data-driven, story-driven, or provocative in your prompt, and the skill will adapt accordingly.

faq.question: Does this skill require Python?
faq.answer: The skill includes an optional Python script for local execution, but it also works as a prompt-based skill without running any code. The AI can generate hooks directly from the templates.

faq.question: What output format does the skill use?
faq.answer: The skill returns a JSON object with a primary hook and an array of alternative hooks. Each output includes the hook type, either opening or closing.

faq.question: Is this skill only for medical presentations?
faq.answer: It is optimized for medical and healthcare topics but the template structure works for any presentation domain. Replace the topic with your subject and the skill will generate appropriate hooks.
